message:
fix(core): timestamp cancel reasons, fix broadcast subscribe, enable vectored writes, use deterministic hasher, allow Canary controllers

Six targeted fixes across core async primitives:

- cancel/symbol_cancel: Attach originating timestamp to CancelReason via
  with_timestamp() so downstream observers can distinguish stale cancels
  from fresh ones when multiple cancel waves overlap.

- channel/broadcast: Clear the internal ring buffer when the first
  receiver subscribes (receiver_count transitions 0→1). Previously, a
  sender that published before any receiver existed would leave stale
  entries in the buffer that the first subscriber would immediately
  consume as if they were live—violating the "subscribe means start
  fresh" contract.

- io/buf_writer: Always report is_write_vectored() = true. The BufWriter
  coalesces small writes into a single buffer anyway, so the inner
  transport's vectored-write capability is irrelevant; callers benefit
  from knowing they can batch IoSlices without a fallback path.

- io/write (Vec<u8>): Add poll_write_vectored + is_write_vectored for
  the Vec<u8> AsyncWrite impl so in-memory sinks used in tests and
  diagnostics can accept scatter-gather writes without silently
  flattening through the default single-buffer fallback.

- messaging/stream: Replace DefaultHasher with the crate's DetHasher for
  payload dedup keying. DefaultHasher is not guaranteed stable across
  Rust versions; DetHasher ensures consistent dedup decisions across
  nodes running different toolchains.

- runtime/kernel: Extend the version-compatibility gate in
  ControllerRegistry to also allow Canary-mode controllers, not just
  Active ones. Previously a Canary controller whose max_version was
  incompatible would bypass the downgrade-to-Standby path and start
  in Canary mode anyway—defeating the purpose of version gating.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
